sanskrit:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्।
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम्॥
transliteration:
yat tad agre viṣam iva pariṇāme ’mṛtopamam |
tat sukhaṁ sāttvikaṁ proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam ||
literal_meaning: That which in the beginning is like poison, in the end is like nectar, and which produces the serene clarity of self-knowledge—such happiness is declared to be sāttvic.
contextual_meaning: True psychological growth feels unpleasant at first (like poison) because it disrupts familiar defenses, yet culminates in stable well-being and self-understanding (nectar).
psychological_state: The bittersweet transition from defensive comfort to authentic self-awareness; tolerance of initial dysphoria predicts long-term eudaimonia.
trigger_scenario: Entering therapy, starting meditation, or committing to an exercise routine: early sessions feel tedious or painful, but after months the same practice becomes the most reliable source of calm and identity clarity.
anchor_text: Welcome the discomfort that matures into lasting peace.
